title: MongoDB and Mongoose ŌĆō Connecting and Modeling
description: Connect to MongoDB, define schemas, and create models with Mongoose.

slides.title: What is Mongoose? ­¤ÉČ
slides.content:
# Mongoose ODM

Mongoose is an **Object Document Mapper (ODM)** for MongoDB and Node.js. It provides:
- A schema-based solution to model data.
- Built-in type casting, validation, and query building.
- Middleware (hooks) for business logic.

Install:
```bash
npm install mongoose
```

slides.title: Connecting to MongoDB ­¤öī
Defining a Schema ­¤ōÉ
Creating a Model ­¤ÅŁ
slides.content:
## Models

A model is a compiled version of the schema ŌĆō it provides an interface to the database collection.

```javascript
const User = mongoose.model('User', userSchema);
// MongoDB collection will be named 'users' (lowercase, plural)
```

Now you can use `User` to perform CRUD operations on the `users` collection.

slides.title: Complete Example

quizData.question: In Mongoose, what does a model represent?

options.text: A collection and provides an interface to it

quizData.explanation: A model is a compiled schema that gives you methods to query and manipulate a specific collection.
